munch-sunset.name: Munch — sunset / anxious
munch-sunset.reference: Edvard Munch, sunset and twilight works
munch-sunset.description: Vivid complementary tensions; reds vs greens, yellows vs deep purples. Designed for warm-hour expressionist landscapes.

kirchner-alpine.name: Kirchner — alpine cool
kirchner-alpine.reference: Ernst Ludwig Kirchner, Davos and Swiss alpine paintings
kirchner-alpine.description: Ultramarine vs cadmium tensions; cool shadows with sharp warm highlights. Suited to cool-hour mountain scenes.

soutine-landscape.name: Soutine — distorted landscape
soutine-landscape.reference: Chaïm Soutine, Céret and Cagnes landscapes
soutine-landscape.description: Earthy umbers, blood-reds, sour greens, ochre highlights. For visceral, gestural landscape moods.

whistler-nocturne.name: Whistler — nocturne
whistler-nocturne.reference: James McNeill Whistler, Nocturnes (Battersea Bridge, Black and Gold, Blue and Silver)
whistler-nocturne.description: Deep blue-greys with whisper-soft gold accents. Smoky midtones; near-monochromatic mood. For night scenes, twilight harbours, low-light architecture.

turner-fog.name: Turner — fog and atmosphere
turner-fog.reference: J.M.W. Turner, late seascapes and Norham Castle
turner-fog.description: Hazy luminous whites and cream-yellows dissolving into faint pinks and greys. Edges blur, sun glows. For overcast, foggy, dawn-mist conditions.

marc-symbolic.name: Marc — symbolic primary
marc-symbolic.reference: Franz Marc (Der Blaue Reiter), Yellow Cow / Blue Horses / Tower of Blue Horses
marc-symbolic.description: Saturated primaries used symbolically: blue = spiritual, yellow = feminine warmth, red = brutal/material. Strong complementary tensions, emotional rather than naturalistic. For landscapes that should feel charged with meaning.

nolde-storm.name: Nolde — storm and unsettled sea
nolde-storm.reference: Emil Nolde, North Sea seascapes and storm paintings
nolde-storm.description: Raw violets, blood-orange horizons, ocean-deep teals, slashing whites. For landscapes that need violence — storms, dramatic sunsets, threatening sky.
